questionHtml: Using the data from Laetitia’s sample, perform the test at a 5% significance level to determine whether Laetitia’s belief is reasonable.
markschemeHtml:
let Q be the random variable the number who go holiday abroad

Q~B200, 0.42                    (A1)

PQ≤67= 0.00850 =0.00849906…                    A1

0.00850<0.05                    R1

EITHER

there is evidence that Laetitia’s claim is reasonable                    A1

OR

there is insufficient evidence to accept the newspaper’s claim                    A1

Note: Follow through within this part, for correctly comparing and concluding with their probability, e.g. it is possible to award A0A0R1A1.

The conclusion to part (e)(iii) MUST follow through from their hypotheses seen in part (e)(ii); if hypotheses are incorrect/reversed etc., the answer to part (e)(iii) must reflect this in order for the A1 to be credited.

[4 marks]

meta.title: Using the data from Laetitia’s sample, perform the test at a 5% significance level to determine whether Laetitia’s belief is reasonable.
breadcrumbLabels: AHL 4.18—T and Z test, type I and II errors
